data.title: DFS Furniture Target Started at 300p by Panmure Liberum
DFS Furniture Started at Buy by Panmure Liberum
B&S International Settles Lawsuit for HK$9.75 Million
data.description:
B & S International Holdings said its subsidiary Wise Fine has settled a legal dispute with LHM for HK$9.75 million, following mediation, according to a Wednesday Hong Kong bourse filing.

The settlement, signed on June 4, ends all claims and counterclaims from a pair of lawsuits dating back to 2021.

The group had already set aside HK$9.25 million in payables related to the case. The extra HK$500,000 payment will not have a material impact on its financials, the filing said.

data.title: XPeng Strikes Partnership With Huawei
data.description:
XPeng will partner with Huawei's Intelligent Automotive Solution unit, with the details set to be announced on Thursday, according to a promotional poster.

A pre-launch posted showed both XPeng and Huawei will co-develop an augmented reality head-up display or AR-HUD system, China Securities Journal reported Wednesday.

The development comes after XPeng Chairman and Chief Executive Officer He Xiaopeng visited Huawei founder Ren Zhengfei, The Standard reported separately the same day.

data.title: Gold Prices May Remain Between $3,100-$3,500/oz This Year — Market Talk
data.description: The gold market has likely transitioned to a higher price regime north of $3,000/oz for the rest of this year, strategists at State Street Global Advisors say. Even if global trade tensions moderate, State Street's base case forecast suggests gold can sustain record price levels between $3,100-$3,500/oz this year. "Our bull case scenario (30% probability) sees gold approaching $4,000/oz over the next six to nine months under certain macroeconomic conditions, including stagflation and accelerated de-dollarization." The strategists see several themes as supporting a bullish gold price environment, such as the potential for higher gold ETF inflows, a rebound in Chinese consumer demand for gold and expectations for the Fed to cut rates. Spot gold is down 0.2% at $3,367.95/oz. (monica.gupta@wsj.com)

data.title: Wisdom Marine Lines's Pre-Tax Income Drops 23% in May
data.description:
Wisdom Marine Lines pre-tax net income slumped 23.4% to $12.5 million, or $0.51 per share in May.

Revenue came in at $41.3 million, down 29.6% year-on-year, amid a prolonged market slump, according to a Wednesday Taiwan Exchange filing.

data.title: India Stocks Extend Gains
data.description:
The BSE Sensex rose 389 points, or 0.5%, to 81,387 in morning trade on Thursday, extending gains from the previous session amid hopes of interest rate cuts by the RBI at its monetary policy meeting on Friday.

The central bank is expected to cut its key repo rate by 25 basis points to 5.75%, due to easing inflation, which would bring the rate to its lowest level since August 2022.

All sectors traded in the green, led by oil & gas and technology.

Meanwhile, small- and mid-cap stocks also advanced, rising by 0.6% and 0.3%, respectively.

Easing US Treasury yields and a weakening US dollar also supported the Sensex, while traders continued to monitor ongoing US-China trade tensions.

The Nifty 50 also gained 0.5%, rising above 24,700, with notable gains in Adani Ports (1.5%), Power Grid (1.4%), Reliance Industries (1.3%), Mahindra & Mahindra (1.2%), and HDFC Bank (0.8%).

data.title: U.S.-Vietnam Trade Talks Set Ahead of Tariff Deadline
data.description:
The U.S. and Vietnam will hold a new round of trade talks next week after Hanoi responded to U.S. trade requests.

Officials did not specify the timing or location of the third round, but pressure is mounting ahead of a pause on 46% "reciprocal" tariffs on Vietnamese exports expiring in early July.

The announcement followed a Wednesday's meeting in Paris between Vietnamese trade minister Nguyen Hong Dien and U.S. Trade Representative Jamieson Greer, where they agreed to speed up technical discussions before negotiations "scheduled to be held in the first half of June."
Vietnamese officials described U.S. trade demands as "tough," including measures to reduce reliance on Chinese imports.

Hanoi responded but did not reveal its proposals.

The Trump administration set a deadline for trade negotiations, pushing countries to submit their best offers.

Vietnam reiterated its "determination and goodwill" in resolving outstanding issues but did not detail what remains unresolved.

data.title: Paragon REIT to Delist on June 6
data.description: Paragon REIT will be delisted from the official list of the Singapore Exchange Securities Trading, effective June 6, according to a filing with the Singapore Exchange on Wednesday.

data.title: Welspun Corp Secures New Export Order
data.description:
Welspun Corp has bagged a repeat export order from an undisclosed amount for the supply of 50 km of LSAW pipes and bends, with anti-corrosion and CWC coating, according to a Thursday filing to the Indian stock exchanges.

The order is for a critical offshore project in the Middle East.

Since early May, the company has received additional orders worth a total of 4.50 billion Indian rupees for its India pipes facility.

These orders will be executed in the financial year 2026-27.

The company's shares were up nearly 1% in recent trade.

data.title: UOB-Kay Hian Purchases Additional Shares in Thai Subsidiary
data.description:
UOB-Kay Hian , through its unit UOB-Kay Hian (BVI), acquired 316,000 additional shares in its Thai subsidiary, UOB Kay Hian Securities Thailand (SET:UOBKH), at 5.2471 baht apiece.

Following the purchase, the total number of shares held directly by UOB-Kay Hian in the Thai entity remains unchanged at 70.65%, according to a Wednesday filing with the Singapore Exchange.

data.title: A producer from Saudi Arabia offers Polystyrene (PS) in Mozambique
data.description: A Polymerupdate source in East Africa reported that a Saudi Arabian producer is heard to have announced its offer for PS grade at the USD 1390/mt levels.This offer is for June 2025 shipment on CFR Beira port basis.

data.title: West African Resources' Shift to New Mining Code Has Limited Financial Impact, Says Euroz Hartleys
data.description:
West African Resources is expected to have limited impact from its decision to align with the new Burkina Faso Mining Code, which increases the government's free-carried project interest to 15% from 10%, according to a Wednesday Euroz Hartleys note.

The company, on Wednesday, also confirmed its gold production guidance for the year of 190,000 to 210,000 ounces at an all-in sustaining cost of less than $1,350 per ounce.

The change to 85% from 90% ownership slightly lowers the company's net asset value by 3.2% to AU$3.94 per share, but the overall financial impact is limited, the note said.

The research firm noted that the change in ownership also aligns aligns to neighbouring Guinea's 15% free-carry, and Tanzania's 16% state free-carry, which have both economic and social benefits.

Euroz Hartleys maintained the company's buy rating with a reduced price target of AU$4, down from AU$4.10.

The company's shares fell past 3% in recent Thursday trade.

data.title: Citycon to Repurchase EUR100 Million Notes Following Tender Offer
data.description:
Citycon said Wednesday it plans to repurchase 100 million euros in aggregate principal amount of its 300 million-euro 2.375% guaranteed notes due 2027.

The urban and commercial real estate company received valid tenders for 128.6 million euros in aggregate nominal amount. The offer was launched on May 27 and expired on Tuesday.

Settlement is expected on Friday.

data.title: India's Newgen Software Technologies jumps on order win
data.description:
** Shares of Newgen Software Technologies jump 3.5% to 1,269 rupees

** Order for enterprise workflow & content management system - NEWG

** Over 2.3 million shares traded, 1.2x 30-day moving avg

** Stock down 25% YTD

data.title: Japan's 30-year bonds rise after weak auction outcome, in line with expectations
data.description:
Japan's 30-year government bond prices rose after an auction of the notes saw its weakest outcome in one and a half years, in line with market expectations.

The bid-to-cover ratio, a measure of demand that gauges the number of bids against the amount of securities on offer, fell to 2.921, the worst since December 2023, and was down from 3.074 at the prior sale in May.

The auction followed a weak outcome of the 40-year bond sale last week, and was a gauge for demand in so-called super-long bonds, which saw heavy sell-offs in May.

"The weak outcome signalled the market has not recovered from the weakness, with volatilities in yields remaining high," said Katsutoshi Inadome, senior strategist at Sumitomo Mitsui Trust Asset Management.

The poor auction results would add pressure on the finance ministry to reduce the issuance of bonds with super-long maturities.

Such expectations rose after Reuters reported last week the Ministry of Finance is considering reducing its sales of super-long bonds.

The ministry could reduce the sale amounts as early as July, after hearing opinions from primary dealers at a meeting scheduled later this month, strategists said.

Sentiment will be weak as long as the scale of expected cuts remains unclear, they said.

The 30-year JGB yield (JP30YTN=JBTC) was last down 7 basis points at 2.875%, extending the decline of 5.5 bps before the auction. Bond yields move inversely to prices.

"The yields fell so much ahead of the auction because some investors bought the 30-year bonds, expecting that the auction outcome would be good," Inadome said.

The 10-year JGB yield (JP10YTN=JBTC) fell 3.5 bps to 1.465% and the five-year yield (JP5YTN=JBTC) fell 2 bps to 1.02%.

data.title: United Renewable Energy to Phase Out PERC Production Lines
data.description:
United Renewable Energy will phase out its PERC production lines as part of a strategic shift.

The company is focusing on TOPCon technology and energy storage to stay competitive amid changing market trends, according to a Wednesday Taiwan Exchange filing.

The decommissioning process will occur in two phases and will be completed by the fourth quarter.

Affected employees will be offered internal transfers or severance and career transition support per labor laws, the solar energy company added.

data.title: Saudi Regulator OKs Aditum Capital's New Business License
data.description:
Saudi Arabia's Capital Market Authority granted Aditum Capital Co. a license to conduct managing investment and operating funds activities in the securities business.

The license issuance follows the completion of all regulatory and business requirements by the investment management company, according to a Wednesday notice.

data.title: Far East Orchard Terminates Joint Venture Agreement with Real Hospitality Group Asia
data.description:
Far East Orchard subsidiary, Far East Hospitality Management Asia terminated a joint venture agreement with Real Hospitality Group Asia, according to a Wednesday filing with the Singapore Exchange.

After initially intending to incorporate a JVCo in Hong Kong and an Opco in China, the partners have not been able to source opportunities that aliged with the objectives.

data.title: Kurabo Industries Faces 3.66 Billion Yen Damages Lawsuit Over 2022 Logistics Facility Fire
data.description:
Kurabo Industries has been sued by Sompo Japan Insurance for 3.66 billion yen in damages under insurance subrogation for a 2022 fire at a logistics facility, according to a Wednesday filing on the Tokyo Stock Exchange.

The lawsuit, filed March 31 and received by the company on June 3, stems from a fire at SBS Flec Co.'s Ami No. 2 Logistics Center, where Kurabo's Chemical Products Division had conducted heat-insulating work.

Sompo Japan, having paid insurance claims to SBS Flec, is seeking recovery from Kurabo and two other parties, the filing said.

Kurabo said the potential financial impact is unclear.

A separate lawsuit filed earlier by SBS Flec over the same incident remains pending, the company said in the filing.

data.title: BoJ Weighs Slower Bond Taper to Ease Market Volatility
data.description:
The Bank of Japan (BoJ) is considering slowing the pace of its bond purchase tapering from next fiscal year to prevent market disruptions, Reuters reported, citing four sources.

The move follows recent volatility in the Japanese government bond (JGB) market, with super-long yields spiking amid concerns over Japan’s worsening public finances.

While some BoJ officials favor maintaining the current tapering pace to reduce the bank’s market presence, others see merit in slowing down.

A decision is expected at the central bank’s June 16–17 policy meeting, where the board will review its current plan and set a new one for April 2026–March 2027.

Market participants have requested the taper size be cut to around 200 billion yen per quarter—half of the current 400 billion yen.

While the BoJ still holds large JGB volumes, recent volatility has raised concerns about tapering too aggressively.

The central bank aims to avoid destabilizing the yield curve.

data.title: White Cliff Minerals Releases Final Assay Results at Canada Copper Project
data.description:
White Cliff Minerals shared the final assay results from its initial drilling program at the Danvers area within the Rae copper project in Nunavut, Canada, and will begin an expanded multi-rig drill campaign following early site remobilization approval, according to a Thursday filing with the Australian bourse.

Assay results included 105 meters grading 2.25% copper and 6.97 grams per tonne grade of silver from a depth of 27.43 meters. This comprised an 18-meter interval grading 3.17% copper and 6.27 g/t silver from 35.05 meters, along with an additional 35 meters at 3.93% copper and 13.35 g/t silver from 60.96 meters, the filing said.

The results also define a shallow copper-silver mineralized zone extending along a 150-meter northeast-southwest strike and confirm vertical continuity beyond 175 meters, with mineralization still open in all directions.

Approval has been received for earlier mobilization, with the company now expecting to return to the site in the coming weeks to begin follow-up drilling at Danvers and regional exploration, the filing added.

data.title: Nera Telecommunications to Cease Quarterly Reporting
data.description:
Nera Telecommunications said it is not required to report its unaudited financial results on a quarterly, according to a filing with the Singapore Exchange on Wednesday.

Accordingly, the company will cease quarterly reporting and will be reporting the financial results on a half-yearly basis.

The company had been issuing quarterly business updates on a voluntary basis previously.

data.title: Upbeat BidCo Increases Offer Price for French Digital Music Company Believe
data.description:
Upbeat BidCo increased its offer price for Believe to 17.20 euros per share from 15.30 euros for its remaining minority shareholders.

French digital music company Believe's board said the offer is in the interests of minority shareholders by offering immediate liquidity, according to a Wednesday release. The board also received a fairness opinion from Finexsi - Expert & Conseil Financier.

data.title: Cube's All-Store Sales Surge 11% in May
data.description:
Cube's all-store sales increased 11% in May, bringing its cumulative sales growth to 5.4% year on year, according to its Wednesday filing on the Tokyo Stock Exchange.

The company's e-commerce sales for all stores recorded a 1.1% decline, while physical store sales jumped 18% on year in May.

Year to date, all stores' e-commerce sales fell 9% but physical stores gained 13.9%.

For existing stores, May sales rose 4.9% while year-to-date sales slipped 0.8%.

data.title: India's Honasa Consumer rises after tribunal approves merger with subsidiaries
data.description:
** Beauty and personal care company Honasa Consumer up as much as 3.4% to 329.5 rupees, last up ~1%

** Mamaearth-parent gets National Company Law Tribunal approval to merge with its subsidiaries Fusion Cosmeceutics and Just4Kids Services

** Fusion retails the Dr. Sheth's skin care brand

** Merger to prevent cost duplication, improve financial efficiency and co's competitive position, says Honasa

** With session's gains, HONA shares up ~26% YTD
